questions: Should the auto-scroll behavior differ between horizontal (time axis) and vertical (track axis)? For instance, vertical scrolling might want a lower max speed since tracks are only 36px tall, while horizontal could stay at 12px/frame.

Should the left track-label column (144px, sticky) be excluded from the left-edge trigger zone? i.e., auto-scroll left should only trigger when the pointer is near the visible timeline grid edge, not over the labels.
Is there a time-ruler or header area at the top that should similarly be excluded from the top-edge trigger zone?
success_criteria: Dragging a clip near any edge of the timeline scroll container causes the container to scroll in that direction.
Scroll speed accelerates as the pointer moves closer to the edge (not constant speed).
External file/asset/text-tool drops trigger auto-scroll when dragged near edges during dragover.
Marquee selection extends correctly when the scroll container auto-scrolls (selection rectangle grows to encompass newly-visible clips).
Auto-scroll stops immediately when the drag/drop/selection ends (no lingering scroll).
Auto-scroll stops when the pointer moves away from the edge zone back into the center.
The drop-position indicator and snap calculations remain correct during auto-scrolling (no stale position bugs).
No visible jank or performance degradation during auto-scroll (single RAF loop, no duplicate timers).
assumptions: The existing `createAutoScroller` utility is the correct foundation — we'll extend it if needed (e.g., insets) rather than rewriting.
The `.timeline-canvas-edit-area` element is the single scroll container for both axes. No separate horizontal/vertical scroll containers exist.
The DragCoordinator re-reads `getBoundingClientRect()` on each `update()` call, so scrolling between updates won't cause stale position data. (Will verify in Step 3.)
40px edge zone and 12 px/frame max speed are reasonable starting defaults. We'll tune after manual testing.
The coordinator's `editAreaRef` is accessible from `useExternalDrop` (either directly passed or available on the coordinator object). If not, we'll resolve it via querySelector like useClipDrag.
Left-edge insets for the sticky label column are likely needed but will be confirmed in Step 5 before implementing.
